validations.recommended_action: ACTION: Remediate 17 non-compliant resources. Review resource configurations against FedRAMP requirements. Current compliance: 46% (Target: ≥80%)
validations.requirement: Validates the complete FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope (MAS) covering machine resources, human resources, and process resources. This rule enforces Structural Boundaries (VPC ID) for compute assets, validates peripheral assets (S3, RDS, ALB), enumerates privileged human access (IAM), and confirms governance processes exist.
validations.implementation_summary: Meridian maintains a single-VPC architecture with all production resources (5 EC2 instances, 1 RDS database, 11 S3 buckets, 1 ALB) explicitly enumerated and validated daily. Human resources are inventoried via IAM user and role enumeration, with security-relevant roles (Admin, Security, Incident Response) specifically tracked. Process resources are validated by confirming the existence of governance procedures in the security-governance CodeCommit repository. Our small footprint enables complete boundary visibility across machine, human, and process resources.
validations.justification: Validates the Live FedRAMP Boundary (MAS) across all three resource types: 1) Machine resources — the four critical asset classes (VPC boundary, EC2 compute, RDS data, S3 storage, ALB network), 2) Human resources — privileged IAM users/roles with production access, 3) Process resources — governance documentation defining security procedures.
validations.pass_criteria: PASS if: A dedicated non-default VPC exists as the production boundary, at least one running EC2 instance operates within it, at least one RDS database instance is provisioned, S3 buckets exist for storage, at least one load balancer provides ingress/egress, IAM users and security-relevant roles are enumerated, and security governance procedures exist in CodeCommit.
validations.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No non-default VPC exists, no running EC2 instances are found, no RDS instances are provisioned, no S3 buckets exist, no load balancers are configured, IAM enumeration fails, or security governance procedures are missing. Any missing resource class (machine, human, or process) means the Minimum Assessment Scope is incomplete.
validations.cli_command: aws ec2 describe-vpcs --filters Name=isDefault,Values=false --output json; aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=instance-state-name,Values=running --output json; aws rds describe-db-instances --query "DBInstances[*].{ID:DBInstanceIdentifier, Engine:Engine, VPC:DBSubnetGroup.VpcId}" --output json; aws s3api list-buckets --output json; aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[*].{ARN:LoadBalancerArn, DNS:DNSName, VPC:VpcId}" --output json; aws iam list-users --query "Users[*].{UserName:UserName, CreateDate:CreateDate, PasswordLastUsed:PasswordLastUsed}" --output json; aws iam list-roles --query "Roles[?contains(RoleName, 'Admin') || contains(RoleName, 'Security') || contains(RoleName, 'Incident')]" --output json; aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path procedures/ --output json

command_executions.description: BOUNDARY: Validate the dedicated Production VPC (The Structural Boundary of the MAS).
command_executions.command: aws ec2 describe-vpcs --filters Name=isDefault,Values=false --output json

command_executions.description: COMPUTE: Validate that all active EC2 instances reside within the defined VPC Boundary.
command_executions.command: aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=instance-state-name,Values=running --output json

command_executions.description: DATABASE: Validate RDS instances to ensure federal data is contained within the boundary.
command_executions.command: aws rds describe-db-instances --query "DBInstances[*].{ID:DBInstanceIdentifier, Engine:Engine, VPC:DBSubnetGroup.VpcId}" --output json

command_executions.description: STORAGE: Validate S3 buckets (Peripheral Assets) that fall within the assessment scope.
command_executions.command: aws s3api list-buckets --output json

command_executions.description: NETWORK: Validate Load Balancers (The Ingress/Egress points of the boundary).
command_executions.command: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[*].{ARN:LoadBalancerArn, DNS:DNSName, VPC:VpcId}" --output json

command_executions.description: HUMAN: Enumerate IAM users with production account access (privileged human resources in the MAS).
command_executions.command: aws iam list-users --query "Users[*].{UserName:UserName, CreateDate:CreateDate, PasswordLastUsed:PasswordLastUsed}" --output json

command_executions.description: HUMAN-ROLES: Enumerate security-relevant IAM roles (ISSO, SysAdmin, IR roles) mapped to human resource responsibilities.
command_executions.command: aws iam list-roles --query "Roles[?contains(RoleName, 'Admin') || contains(RoleName, 'Security') || contains(RoleName, 'Incident')]" --output json

command_executions.description: PROCESS: Validate that security governance procedures exist (process resources in the MAS).
command_executions.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path procedures/ --output json

validations.requirement: Set security goals and develop automated validation of status and progress.
validations.implementation_summary: Security goals are tracked via unified_ksi_validations.json, auto-generated by GitHub Actions and published to our Trust Center API. All 61 KSIs are evaluated daily with results publicly accessible.
validations.justification: Validates the Active Compliance Tracker. Checks for the 'unified_ksi_validations.json' artifact in the S3 compliance bucket. This file represents the aggregated status of all Phase 2 KSIs, proving that security goals are actively tracked and validated.

validations.justification: Validates VDR Outputs. Instead of just checking if scanners are 'on', this validates the 'assessed_vulnerabilities_integrated.json' and 'vdr_vulnerability_status.json' artifacts. These prove that vulnerability data is being actively collected, integrated, and tracked.

validations.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Either vulnerability artifact is missing from S3, or the vulnerability management policy document does not exist in the security-governance repository.

validations.recommended_action: ACTION: Remediate 4 non-compliant resources. Review resource configurations against FedRAMP requirements. Current compliance: 33% (Target: ≥80%)
validations.requirement: Integrate FedRAMP's Incident Communications Procedures (ICP) into incident response procedures and infrastructure.
validations.implementation_summary: ICP integration uses GitHub Actions for automated FedRAMP/CISA notification within 1 hour of incident detection. Playbooks in CodeCommit define escalation paths for Critical, High, and standard incidents.
validations.justification: Validates ICP Readiness. Checks for: 1) The ICP Playbook and IR Plan (Governance), and 2) Dedicated Security SNS Topics (Infrastructure). Filtering the SNS topics specifically for 'security', 'incident', or 'alert' proves that a dedicated communication channel exists for high-priority events.
validations.pass_criteria: PASS if: Both the ICP playbook and incident response plan exist in CodeCommit, at least one dedicated security/incident/alert SNS topic exists, AND those topics have active subscriptions (email, SMS, or Lambda) to ensure alert delivery.
validations.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Either governance document (ICP playbook or IR plan) is missing, no dedicated security SNS topics exist, or the topics have zero active subscriptions (alerts would not be delivered).

command_executions.description: GOVERNANCE: Validate the FedRAMP Incident Communications Procedure (ICP) Playbook.
command_executions.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/icp_playbook.md --output json

command_executions.description: GOVERNANCE: Validate the master Incident Response Plan.
command_executions.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/incident-response-plan.md --output json

command_executions.description: INFRASTRUCTURE: Validate existence of dedicated SNS topics for Security Incidents.
command_executions.command: aws sns list-topics --query "Topics[?contains(TopicArn, 'security') || contains(TopicArn, 'incident') || contains(TopicArn, 'alert')]" --output json

command_executions.description: REACHABILITY: Validate that the security topics have active subscriptions (email/SMS/Lambda) to ensure alerts are actually delivered.
command_executions.command: aws sns list-subscriptions --query "Subscriptions[?contains(TopicArn, 'security') || contains(TopicArn, 'incident') || contains(TopicArn, 'alert')]" --output json
